auth.setupDescription: Set the email and password that will protect this dashboard.
auth.loginDescription: Sign in to manage your keys, routing, and analytics.
auth.serverUnreachableBefore: Can't reach the server. Make sure the backend is running (
auth.setupCodeHint: Setting up from another device needs the one-time code printed in the server logs at startup.

models.title: Models
models.mediaEmpty: No models yet. They sync from the catalog.
models.toolsTitle: Emits structured tool calls, so it is eligible for tool-bearing requests
models.modelNotFound: This model is not available, or it has no configured providers.
models.providerIdsHint: Send the unified model id to fail over across every provider, or a provider-specific id to pin one provider.
models.settingsHint: Local edits to catalog models are kept across catalog syncs. Deleted catalog models stay deleted until you add them again through config.
models.aggregateBudgetTitle: Total free tokens per month across this model's providers
models.rateTitle: Rate-limited — no fixed monthly token budget
models.unifyToggleHint: Show a model served by several providers as one item, and fail over across its providers when it's selected.
models.noModelsBefore: No models available. Add API keys on the

strategies.title: Routing strategy
strategies.description: Pick a routing strategy. In Manual mode you drag to set the order; the other strategies route by live score across reliability, speed and intelligence.
strategies.guardrailsTooltip: Always-on guardrails: free-quota headroom × live rate-limit penalty. Below 1.0 means the model is being held back.
strategies.scoreTooltip: Final routing score = weighted average of the three axes, multiplied by the guardrails. Higher routes first.
strategies.modeManualHint: Manual mode: requests follow the order below, top-to-bottom. Drag to reorder.
strategies.modeScoreHint: Scores update from live traffic. The order below is how requests are routed right now.
strategies.manualBlurb: Route in the exact order you set below. Drag the handles to reorder. No scoring; the chain is followed top-to-bottom.
strategies.balancedBlurb: Reliability leads (50%), with speed and intelligence weighted equally (25% each). A sensible all-round default.
strategies.smartestBlurb: Prefer the most capable model that still works. Intelligence 55%, reliability 35%, speed 10%.
strategies.fastestBlurb: Prefer the fastest model that still works. Speed 55%, reliability 35%, intelligence 10%.
strategies.reliableBlurb: Maximize success rate above all. Reliability 70%, speed and intelligence 15% each.
strategies.customBlurb: Set your own balance of reliability, speed and intelligence with sliders. Same engine as the presets, just your weights.
strategies.customWeightsHelp: Sliders are independent; shares auto-balance to 100%.
strategies.weightRequired: At least one weight must be above zero.

penaltyInspector.title: Router pressure
penaltyInspector.subtitle: Penalties, cooldowns, and failures from the last {minutes} min

keys.pageDescription: Provider credentials and the unified API key your apps connect with.
keys.endpointEmbeddingsHint: model: "auto" or a family from the Embeddings tab
keys.anthropicDesc: Point a Claude or Anthropic SDK client at this server. Anthropic clients send Claude model names — map each to Auto (the router picks a free model) or pin a specific one.
keys.anthropicAuto: Auto — router picks the best model
keys.anthropicNote: Anthropic clients keep their built-in Claude model names; the mapping above decides how each one routes into your pool.
keys.exportHealthyOnlyDesc: Exclude keys that are currently failing health checks
keys.label: Label
keys.outboundProxyDescription: Route outbound LLM requests through a proxy. Supports SOCKS5, HTTP, and HTTPS.
keys.addCustomDescription: Point chat, embedding, image, or audio models at any OpenAI-compatible endpoint. The API key is optional for local servers.
keys.deleteKeyConfirm: Delete this key? This cannot be undone.
keys.unifiedKeyDescAfter: ; it authenticates requests to this proxy.
keys.serverUnreachableAfter: . Make sure the backend is running. npm run dev starts both, and the server logs print under the server prefix.
keys.keylessHint: No API key needed: this provider's free tier is anonymous (rate-limited per IP).
keys.noProviderKeys: No provider keys yet. Add one above to start routing.
keys.proxyEnvHintAfter: environment variable (takes precedence). Leave blank to disable. Examples:
keys.importKeysDescription: Upload .env, JSON, JSONC, CSV, Markdown, or text files.
keys.noPreviewKeys: No keys found in those files.
keys.noImportSelection: Select at least one row with a provider.
keys.duplicatesFound: {count} duplicate(s) detected — already exist and will be skipped.
keys.quotaSignalsEmptyDesc: This fills in after providers return rate-limit headers, quota errors, or validation signals.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.description: Request volume, latency, token usage, and failures.
analytics.ttftByProvider: Time to first token by provider
analytics.ttftEmpty: No time to first token recorded yet.
analytics.savingsHint: You actually saved ${actual} over the last {range}. That is what the same tokens would have cost on paid APIs, priced per model. The number shown projects your pace from the last {span} of data to a full 30 days.
analytics.savingsHintExact: You actually saved ${actual} over the last {range}. That is what the same tokens would have cost on paid APIs, priced per model. The number shown is your real 30-day total.
analytics.requestsHintPinned: {pinned} of these requests pinned a specific model by name. {honored} were served by the pinned model; {failed} failed over to a different one. The rest were auto-routed.
analytics.requestsHintAuto: All requests in this period were auto-routed; no client pinned a specific model by name.

playground.title: Playground
playground.description: Send a chat completion through the router and see which provider serves it.
playground.inputPlaceholder: Type a message… (⏎ to send, ⇧⏎ for newline)
playground.emptyTitle: Send a message to get started.
playground.emptyDescription: Using {model}. Switch models in the selector above.
playground.noModels: No models yet. Add an API key on the Keys page to populate this list.
playground.systemPromptPlaceholder: Optional. Sent as a system message before your chat.

embeddings.title: Models
embeddings.description: Embeddings fail over within a family only: the same model served by another provider. Vectors from different models are incompatible, so the router never swaps models on you.
embeddings.autoDescription: model: "auto" on POST /v1/embeddings routes to the default family. Naming a family (or a provider model id) pins that family; providers inside it are tried in order.
embeddings.emptyDesc: Embedding families appear here once you add a provider key that supports embeddings.

fusion.title: Fusion
fusion.description: A panel of models answers in parallel, then a judge synthesizes one stronger answer. Use it by calling the API with model "fusion".
fusion.panelSourceHelp: Where the panel models come from when a request doesn't specify its own.

mode.autoHelp: Pick a diverse set of models from your Fallback Chain, ranked by your active routing strategy.
mode.explicitHelp: Always use the specific models you choose below.

fusion.noModels: No models are connected yet. Add an API key, then enable models in the Fallback Chain.
fusion.panelSizeHelp: How many diverse models to pick automatically (1–{max}).
fusion.judgeHelp: The model that synthesizes the panel's answers into one. Auto uses your top-ranked available model.
fusion.strategySynthesize: Synthesize — blend the panel into one answer
fusion.strategyBestOf: Best of — return the single strongest answer (no judge call)
fusion.exposePanelHelp: Attach each model's answer and the judge metadata under an x_fusion field in the response.
fusion.howToUseHelp: Send a normal chat request with model "fusion". These settings are the default; any request can override them with a "fusion" field.

premium.title: Premium
premium.description: The live model catalog, on every device.
premium.liveDescription: New free models, quota changes, and quirk fixes land here within hours of being shipped. The app checks automatically twice a day; nothing to do.
premium.snapshotDescription: Free tier: the catalog refreshes from a monthly snapshot, checked automatically twice a day. Premium switches this to the live feed, updated every 2-3 days.
premium.willNotRenew: Will not renew. Premium until {date}, then this install falls back to the free monthly catalog.
premium.keyInactive: This key is no longer active. The app keeps working on the free monthly catalog.
premium.manageHint: Manage subscription opens Stripe's billing portal: cancel, update your card, or download invoices. Removing the key only deactivates this device; your purchase is untouched.
premium.keyHint: Your key is in your purchase email and on the post-checkout page. Lost it?
premium.upsellDescription: New free models the moment they exist, instead of 30 days later. One key, every device. Cancel anytime; the router stays free forever.

errors.boundaryDescription: The dashboard hit an unexpected error. Your data is safe. Try again or go back to the models page.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: This page does not exist or may have moved.

setup.title: Getting started
setup.subtitle: Three steps to route your first request.
setup.step1Desc: Connect at least one provider account. Free keys work.
setup.step2Desc: Pick any model in the Playground and say hello.
setup.step3Desc: Use your unified API key with any OpenAI-compatible client.

validation.url: Enter a valid URL starting with http:// or https://
